untranslatable_reason: Signal requires cointegration testing (statistical relationship between two instruments), which is not available in the allowed indicator set. The backtest engine appears to be single-instrument focused. Cointegration detection requires bivariate analysis (ADF test on spread of two paired instruments) with parameters: lookback_period, threshold, and half_life—all marked AUTHOR_SILENT. Used zscore_20 as a proxy for spread deviation from mean, but this cannot validate the underlying cointegrating relationship or confirm stationarity. Pair selection logic and cointegration validation are not expressible.
translation_notes: Attempted approximation using z-score of spread as a proxy for mean reversion, but true cointegration pairs trading requires (1) identification of cointegrating pairs via Johansen test or ADF test on spread, (2) spread calculation between two instruments, (3) statistical validation of stationarity—none of which are supported by single-instrument indicators. This rule is fundamentally incompatible with the available indicator set without a dedicated cointegration/pairs module.
